revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:フネ古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Fune Kofun | image =File:Fune Kofun (フネ古墳).jpg | location = {{ill|Suwa|qid=Q846338|1=Suwa, Nagano}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.999|N|138.11605555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | iso = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (estimated) | area = 15 m (E-W) × 25 m (N-S) | constructed = Early 5th century | burial = Split-bamboo-style wooden coffins (2) | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = [[Snake-shaped sword]], [[Plain-ring pommel sword]], {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} (zoomorphic pattern), [[Straight sword]], {{ill|Utility knife|en|Utility knife|qid=Q23769|lt=Utility knife|1=Utility knife}}, agricultural tools, etc. | Historic Site = {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Treasures|ja|長野県cultural property一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (burial goods) | map_dot_label = Fune Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | map_type = 方墳 | architectural_styles= {{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353|1=Square Kofun}} }}
'''Fune Kofun''' (フネ古墳) is an early 5th-century Kofun (''kofun'') located in the Nakasu district of {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|qid=Q846338|lt=Suwa|1=Suwa, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It is the oldest ''kofun'' in the Suwa region.
== Overview == The ''kofun'' was discovered in 1959 during agricultural work. Its name derives from the local place name "Fune" (舟). Situated on a hill near the base of {{ill|Mount Moriya|ja|守屋山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Moriya|qq=}}, the site overlooks {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|qid=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}} to the west and offers a view of {{ill|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|en|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|qid=Q905588|lt=Mount Yatsugatake|1=Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group}} to the east. Nearby to the west is the {{ill|Katayama Kofun|ja|片山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The ''kofun'' originally had a flat, elevated area measuring approximately 15 meters east-west and 25 meters north-south. The mound itself has since disappeared. Archaeological excavations led by Eiichi Fujimori revealed two parallel wooden burial chambers oriented north-south. The eastern coffin measured 6 meters in length and 60 cm in width, while the western coffin was 5.5 meters long and 70 cm wide. These were interred at different times, as indicated by their staggered burial positions.
The Kofun is believed to have been a modified square type, contypeing to the terrain. Test excavations in 1990 confirmed a surrounding trench on the south, west, and northwest sides, with natural cliffs on the other sides. The excavation also uncovered broken ''hajiki'' pottery (pedestal cups, small round-bottomed pots, and jar-like vessels) and three iron arrowheads, indicating ritual activities involving pottery smashing in the late 5th century.
Despite its modest size, the ''kofun'' yielded a rich array of grave goods, including tools, weapons, and ornaments. These burial goods included: * '''Eastern coffin:''' Iron swords, knives, and beads. * '''Western coffin:''' Iron axes, sickles, chisels, spears, plain-ring pommel swords, bangles, zoomorphic-pattern bronze mirrors, and deer antler fittings.
Iron burial goods were prevalent, but there were no horse gear or pottery items, setting this ''kofun'' apart. Notably, the use of deer antlers for sword guards and knife handles suggests a connection to regional hunting and ceremonial traditions.
burial goods from the ''kofun'' were Historic Site1 as Nagano Prefectural Treasures in 1990 and are now housed at the {{ill|Suwa City Museum|ja|諏訪市博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa City Museum|1=Suwa City Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85883757}}.
== Interpretation == === Connection to Suwa Faith === The presence of snake-shaped swords and deer antler objects suggests a connection to the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2370082}} and the rituals of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|qid=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The worship of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}, associated with water, wind, and snakes, has long been a central aspect of Suwa's spiritual traditions.
The inclusion of deer-related items aligns with the historical significance of deer in Suwa rituals, including hunting and animal offerings.
=== Occupant === The burial site's location and the symbolic nature of the grave goods point to an individual of considerable influence, likely a local leader who controlled the upper Tenryu River basin and Lake Suwa area.
One theory links the Interred_individual to the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, a family associated with the pre-Yamato deity Moreya. The ''kofun'' reflects the transition from local rule to integration with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court|1=Yamato Kingship}}.
== References == === Sources === {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|qid=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan|1=Suwa clan}} * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|qid=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Dakōken|ja|蛇行剣|1=Dakōken|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dakōken|13=User:Immanuelle/Dakōken|qq=}} * {{ill|Archaeological sites in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archaeological sites in Suwa|1=Suwa, Nagano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q846338}}
== External Links == * [https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3480&seq=0 Fune Kofun burial goods] - Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation (Japanese)
